title: Agreement
description:
A standalone JACS agreement document for verifiable consent to terms. The JACS header owns document identity, versioning, authorship signatures, registration, files, hashes, and visibility; this schema adds agreement-specific terms, parties, policy, consent signatures, evidence references, signed relationship links, and rights metadata. Transcript is an external append-only log, referenced by id; this schema does not store transcript entries inline.

SDK invariants (enforced operationally, not by JSON Schema): (1) transcriptLogRef is set-once at agreement creation and immutable in all successor versions; (2) every agreementSignature's outer agentId / agentVersion / signedAgreementHash / signedVersion / signedAt fields must match the corresponding values bound by the inner JACS signature, and verifiers must check both; (3) signatures from agents not listed in parties are rejected unless signaturePolicy.allowDelegatedSignatures is true AND a verifiable delegationChain resolves to a signer-role party.

jacsType.description: Agreement v2 is a standalone JACS document type, not an embedded agreement sidecar.

jacsLevel.description: Agreements are versioned artifacts: edits create successor versions while preserving signed provenance.

jacsAgreementHash.description: Stable hash of the agreement consent scope. SDKs compute this over: title, description, terms, termsFormat, parties, signaturePolicy, transcriptLogRef, and links (links contribute their signedLinkHash values, NOT their inner signature objects, to avoid circular preimages). NOT over: currentTranscriptHash, evidenceRefs, agreementSignatures, controllers, rights, or any header field. Appending agreementSignatures or growing the external transcript log must not change this hash; swapping transcriptLogRef MUST change it.

title.description: Short human-readable title of the agreement.

description.description: Human-readable summary of the purpose and scope of the agreement.

terms.description: The agreement text the signer parties consent to. MVP is plain text or Markdown; structured terms (action items, deliverables, dates) can be introduced in a later schema version.

status.description: Stored lifecycle status. SDKs must verify this against signatures, signaturePolicy, timeouts, and links rather than trusting it blindly.

parties.description: Agents participating in the agreement. Parties define consent and process roles; they do not replace jacsVisibility read access or controllers edit authority.

agreementSignatures.description: Consent signatures for this agreement. Header jacsSignature means an agent authored this version; entries here mean a party signed the agreement consent scope (signers) or attested to process (witnesses).

transcriptLogRef.description: jacsId of an external append-only JACS document that holds this agreement's transcript (messages, statements, votes, mediation events). The log is referenced by stable id only; its own versioning and hash-chain rules are defined by the transcript-log schema (separate, not in this MVP). Set once at agreement creation; immutable thereafter. INCLUDED in jacsAgreementHash — swapping the log ref invalidates prior signatures.

currentTranscriptHash.description: Optional non-binding checkpoint of the transcript log hash at the time this agreement version was emitted. NOT part of jacsAgreementHash and NOT what signers consent to; each signature records its own signedTranscriptHash for point-in-time evidence visibility. Verifiers compute the authoritative current hash from the external log on demand.

evidenceRefs.description: References to signed JACS documents used as standing evidence: context, attachments, identity proofs, or other static supporting material. Live transcript entries (messages, votes, mediation chat) belong in the external transcript log, not here. Attachments may also be carried by header jacsFiles when they are first-class document files rather than referenced JACS docs.

links.description: Signed relationship links to other agreements. Each link carries its own signedLinkHash and signature to prevent forged supersession or termination claims, and to prevent copy-paste of a link to a different source agreement.

controllers.description: Agent IDs authorized by agreement policy to propose successor versions, append to the transcript log, modify evidenceRefs, change status, or add parties. This is edit authority — distinct from parties (who is bound), jacsVisibility (who can read), and rights.copyrightHolders (who claims IP).

party.description: A participant in an agreement. Signer-role parties consent to the terms and are bound. Witness-role parties attest to process, time, or identity but are not bound. Observer-role parties are listed for visibility but neither sign nor attest. Delegation is expressed on the signature, not as a party role.

agentId.description: JACS agent id for the party.

agentVersion.description: Optional snapshot of the agent identity version expected to sign for this party.

agentType.description: Party type, matching the JACS agent schema vocabulary.

role.description: Agreement role. Only signer-role parties are bound. Witness-role parties attest. Observer-role parties are listed without consent or attestation responsibility.

delegatedBy.description: Optional: agent id of a delegator on whose behalf this party may sign. The actual proof of authority lives in agreementSignature.delegationChain.

signaturePolicy.description: Rules for when the agreement is considered complete. SDKs must reject agreementSignatures from agents not listed in parties unless allowDelegatedSignatures is true and a valid delegation chain resolves to a signer-role party.

partyQuorum.description: Required signer-party consent threshold. 'all' requires every signer-role party; 'majority' requires more than half; an integer N requires at least N signer-role party signatures.

witnessRequired.description: Minimum number of witness-role party signatures required in addition to signer-party quorum. Witnesses attest but do not count toward partyQuorum.

timeout.description: ISO 8601 deadline after which new agreement signatures are not accepted; status advances to 'expired' if quorum was not reached.

requiredAlgorithms.description: If specified, only signatures using one of these algorithms are accepted.

minimumStrength.description: Minimum cryptographic strength tier required for agreement signatures.

allowDelegatedSignatures.description: Whether signatures from a delegate (an agent not listed as a party) are accepted when their delegationChain resolves to a signer-role party. Default-deny: high-trust workflows must opt in.

agreementSignature.description: A scoped agreement consent or attestation signature. The signature object is a standard JACS signature; surrounding fields record what agreement scope this signature was intended to bind and what process evidence the signer had visibility into. SDKs MUST verify that outer fields (agentId, agentVersion, signedAgreementHash, signedVersion, signedAt) match what the inner JACS signature actually binds.

agentId.description: The agent that produced this signature. For non-delegated signatures, must match a party.agentId. For delegated signatures, must resolve through delegationChain to a signer-role party.

agentVersion.description: Agent identity version at the time of signing.

role.description: Role this signature represents. Signer signatures count toward partyQuorum; witness signatures count toward witnessRequired. Observers do not sign.

signedAgreementHash.description: The jacsAgreementHash value this signature binds. Lets verifiers detect post-signature edits to the consent scope.

signedTranscriptHash.description: Optional snapshot of the external transcript log hash visible to the signer at signing time. Not part of consent; records context only.

signedVersion.description: Agreement jacsVersion at the time of signing.

delegationChain.description: If signing on behalf of a party, the ordered list of signed JACS delegation document references proving authority. Each entry is a verifiable {jacsId, jacsVersion, jacsSha256}.

jacsDocumentRef.description: Verifiable reference to a specific signed JACS document version.

evidenceRef.description: Reference to a standing piece of evidence supporting the agreement (context, attachment, identity proof). Live transcript-style entries belong in the external transcript log, not here.

kind.description: How this evidence supports the agreement.

description.description: Optional human-readable note about this evidence.

agreementLink.description: Signed relationship from this agreement to another JACS document (typically another agreement). The signature binds signedLinkHash, which is computed over the link payload (rel + target + reason) PLUS the source agreement's jacsId — this prevents copy-paste of a signed link to a different source agreement, and avoids the circular-preimage problem of trying to hash a link that contains its own signature.

reason.description: Optional human-readable reason for the relationship. Included in signedLinkHash.

signedLinkHash.description: Hash that this link's signature binds. SDKs compute this over: rel, target (jacsId + jacsVersion + jacsSha256), reason (if present), and the SOURCE agreement's jacsId. Excludes signature and signedAt. The agreement-level jacsAgreementHash references this signedLinkHash value for each link, not the inner signature object.

signerAgentId.description: Agent that signed this link. Must be either a controller of the source agreement or a signer-role party.

rights.description: Optional copyright and license metadata. Soft claims, not authorization: does not grant read access, edit authority, or consent. Read access comes from header jacsVisibility; edit authority from controllers; consent from agreementSignatures.

copyrightHolders.description: Agent IDs claiming copyright in the agreement document.

license.description: Optional license identifier or short reference (e.g., 'Apache-2.0', 'Common Clause + Apache-2.0').
